hook: Luca walks back to the Cole House with the camera in his bag and the key in his hand, determined to find the floorboard he saw on the tape.
goal: Locate the room shown on the tape, lift the loose floorboard, and retrieve the metal box hidden beneath it.

start_state.location: Luca's flat (loc_003), about to leave for the Cole House (loc_001)
knowledge: The tape shows a man and woman hiding a metal box under a loose floorboard in a room with pale walls, a window on the left, and dark floorboards — features Luca recognised as matching the Cole House
Ray was insistent at the house that Luca bag and throw away everything without keeping anything
The tape is inside the camera, which Luca still has
suspicions: The box on the tape may still be under the floorboard after fifty years
Ray may know something important about the contents of the house

continuity_rules: Must start after Luca has already watched the tape and picked up the house key — do not replay or re-describe the tape as a new discovery
Must not re-present the tape's contents as a revelation — Luca uses it as a reference tool, not a surprise
The room must match the tape's description: pale walls, window on the left, dark floorboards
The metal box must be exactly where the tape showed it — under a loose floorboard near the far wall
Must not open the box or reveal its contents — it stays locked throughout this episode
key_events: Luca arrives at the Cole House, unlocks the door, and moves through the ground-floor rooms one by one, holding the camera so he can check each room against the tape — he is looking for pale walls and a window on the left.

Most rooms do not match: wrong wall colour, wrong window position; THEREFORE he reaches a room at the back of the house that fits exactly — pale walls, window on the left, dark floorboards — and he puts the camera down.

He gets on his knees and presses each floorboard in turn; most are firm; BUT one near the far wall shifts under his hand — he lifts it and pulls out the metal box from the dark gap below; it is cold and heavy; he tries the lid and it does not open — it is locked; he turns the box over and sees letters scratched into the metal side: COLE.

While he is still kneeling on the floor, his phone rings — it is Ray; Ray asks, in a voice that is too calm, whether Luca found anything of value in the house; Luca says no, there is nothing; Ray says thank you and hangs up before Luca can ask anything; Luca carries the box out of the house and walks back to his flat.
major_reveal: The metal box from the tape is real and still in its hiding place — but it is locked, and the name COLE is scratched into its side, confirming the family's identity for the first time.
new_thread_created: thread_004: Why does Ray Brennan call to ask about 'anything of value' on the exact day Luca finds the box — and how did he know to ask?
tone_guardrails: Ray's phone call must be brief and quiet — suspicion comes from what he does not say, not from dramatic accusation
Only one major revelation: the box is real, locked, and marked COLE
No confrontation — Luca simply lies and leaves; the tension lives in the silence after the call ends
forbidden_moves: Do not re-present the tape's contents as a new discovery — Luca already knows what is on it and uses it as a guide only
Do not open the box or hint at its specific contents
Do not have Ray appear in person — the phone call is his only presence in this episode
Do not explain or reveal how Ray knew to call today
ending_hook: Luca sits at his table with the locked box in front of him; the name COLE means nothing to him yet — but Ray asked about 'anything of value' on the same day Luca found it, and that cannot be a coincidence.
